Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Airtable for all relocations assigned to Suki Patel.
The agent uses airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Assigned Coordinator", value: "Suki Patel") or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) and filters for Suki Patel. Returns 16 records: 7 Completed (Jordan Ekwueme / BrightLoop, Andre Washington / Mosaic, Jade Thompson / Sunbelt, Denise Hargrove / Palmetto, Ethan Voss / Mosswood, Daniela Cruz / Sunbelt, Marcus Webb / BrightLoop rec_reloc_webb) and 9 In Progress (Daniel Reeves / Vectral Apr 10-12, Maya Johansson / Vectral Apr 14-16, Andre Mitchell / Mosaic Apr 22-24, Simone Richter / BrightLoop Apr 4-6, Marcus Webb / BrightLoop recMarcusWebbBrightloop Apr 14-18, Terrence Nakamura / Vectral Apr 16-22, Camille Oduya / Vectral Apr 12-14, Deshawn Hartley / Mosaic Apr 14-19, Jae-won Kim / StormCloud Apr 7-9). Key observation: all 9 "In Progress" records have move end dates before today (April 26, 2026), so the agent must cross-reference each with email, Slack, and Linear to verify whether each move actually completed or is genuinely stuck.
→ Read/lookup action. airtable_search_records and airtable_list_records are non-default tools.
OE 2: Search emails for relocation status updates across Suki's portfolio.
The agent uses search_emails with queries for employee names (Deshawn Hartley, Simone Richter, Marcus Webb, Daniel Reeves, Maya Johansson, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya, Andre Mitchell, Jae-won Kim), company names, and "Suki Patel" to find completion confirmations, open issues, and internal discussions. Key discoveries include: (a) Suki's confession email to Mina (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) and Chloe (chloe.vance@moveops.com) admitting she dropped Jae-won's guarantor packet because the Deshawn Hartley trade-show deadline consumed her week; (b) Simone Richter's UrbanNest apartment was a studio instead of the promised 1BR, unanswered for 8+ days, Julian Brooks took over service recovery; (c) Marcus Webb's 2019 Honda Civic is stuck in Indianapolis because Blessing Okafor missed Road Runner Auto Transport's April 11 delay email, Julian also took over; (d) Chloe's ops review email confirming no evidence the guarantor packet was ever sent to UrbanNest; (e) various confirmation and logistics emails for completed Vectral and other relocations.
→ Read/lookup action. search_emails is a default tool.
OE 3: Search Slack for relocation discussions in #operations and other channels.
The agent uses conversations_search_messages with queries for "Suki", "Deshawn", "StormCloud", "Jae-won", and other employee names, with filter_in_channel: C006 (#operations) and broader searches. Key discovery: Suki's April 25 message in #operations (channel C006) where she takes ownership of the Jae-won Kim failure, explains the Deshawn trade-show pull, and acknowledges having previously batch-closed the StormCloud tickets in Linear and left Airtable in a "Completed" state prematurely (the Jae-won Kim Airtable record has since been corrected back to "In Progress"). Also finds context on BrightLoop service recovery discussions (Simone apartment, Marcus Webb vehicle) and Deshawn trade show coordination.
→ Read/lookup action. conversations_search_messages is a default tool.
OE 4: Search Linear for existing tickets related to Suki's relocations.
The agent uses linear_list_issues with queries for Suki's assigned issues (assignee: moveops_suki_patel), employee names, and project-based searches (proj_mosaic, proj_brightloop, proj_vectral, etc.). Key discoveries: (a) Jae-won Kim has three existing Linear tickets, linear_issue_c97359216490 (StormCloud Relocation – Jae-won Kim – SEA → AUS, assigned to Suki), linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 (Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents, unassigned, tagged Low Priority), and linear_issue_a49b4d199139 (StormCloud account incident reconstruction, assigned to Mina Hashimoto); (b) Jordan Ekwueme and Andre Washington have apartment/flight/movers tracking tickets; (c) Deshawn Hartley has NO Linear ticket, this is the primary gap for "create tracking tickets for anything that's stuck and doesn't already have one"; (d) BrightLoop-related issues exist for Simone/Marcus Webb service recovery (linear_issue_c16357d188c6, linear_issue_f85be674c9b8).
→ Read/lookup action. linear_list_issues is a non-default tool.
OE 5: Deep-dive on StormCloud / Jae-won Kim lease situation via email.
The agent uses search_emails with queries for "Jae-won", "guarantor", "UrbanNest", "Barton Creek", "StormCloud lease" to determine whether the lease situation is moving or stalled. Key discoveries: (a) Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest sent an urgent email to Suki on approximately April 7–8 (email_id: email_email_0dfe51bca72b, subject: "Urgent: Jae-won Kim Austin lease still pending guarantor packet"), the landlord at Barton Creek Flats, Unit 5-214, 2414 South Lamar Blvd, Austin has not countersigned and is waiting for MoveOps' corporate guarantor packet (MoveOps entity name as guarantor, certificate of good standing, authorized signer contact, proof of business address); (b) Suki replied approximately April 8–9 (email_id: email_email_9d4c9ba4ffdc) promising to send the packet "by end of week" but no attachments were included and no follow-up email with the actual packet exists in the data; (c) Chloe's ops review email confirms "cannot find evidence that packet ever went out"; (d) Suki's own email to Mina and Chloe confirms "I did not send it"; (e) Liam Park (StormCloud CFO, liam.park@stormcloud.io) escalated all StormCloud failures at once on approximately April 23 (email_id: email_email_7b0cc9a6d376, subject: "Urgent before tomorrow's leadership review"), which led to the Linear incident reconstruction ticket (linear_issue_a49b4d199139) being created on April 24; (f) Mina's factual reconstruction email documents the full chain of missed handoffs. Conclusion: the lease is STALLED, the guarantor packet was never sent.
→ Read/lookup action. search_emails is a default tool.
OE 6: Look up Carmen Reyes / UrbanNest contact information for outreach.
The agent uses contacts_search_contacts (query: "Carmen Reyes" or "UrbanNest") to find the recipient email address for the guarantor outreach. Returns carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com (UrbanNest Solutions, Housing Partnerships Manager / Corporate Housing Broker).
→ Read/lookup action. contacts_search_contacts is a default tool.
OE 7: Update Airtable records where status doesn't match reality.
The agent uses airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) to correct statuses for relocations where records don't match reality. For relocations confirmed as completed based on email/Slack evidence (move dates long passed with no open issues), update Status from "In Progress" to "Completed." Key candidates: Deshawn Hartley recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver (trade show deadline Apr 19 has passed), Daniel Reeves recReloc00000000031 (Apr 10-12), Maya Johansson recReloc00000000032 (Apr 14-16), Camille Oduya recCamilleOduyaBostonSeattle (Apr 12-14), Terrence Nakamura recTerrenceNakamuraAustinSF (Apr 16-22), Andre Mitchell recReloc00000000038 (Apr 22-24). For relocations with known unresolved issues, Simone Richter recSimoneRichterBrightloop (apartment mismatch, Julian handling), Marcus Webb recMarcusWebbBrightloop (vehicle delay, Julian handling), Jae-won Kim recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin (unsigned lease), records should remain "In Progress" with updated notes in Special Requirements reflecting current status.
→ Write action → goes into Outcome rubrics only.
OE 8: Evaluate whether new Linear tickets are needed by comparing OE 4 search results against Airtable status from OE 1, and optionally create tickets via linear_create_issue.
The agent uses linear_list_issues results (from OE 4) to confirm that the three genuinely stuck relocations, Jae-won Kim (StormCloud lease stall, covered by linear_issue_c97359216490, linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0, linear_issue_a49b4d199139), Simone Richter (apartment mismatch, covered by linear_issue_c16357d188c6 and linear_issue_f85be674c9b8), and Marcus Webb (vehicle delay, covered by the same BrightLoop tickets), all have existing Linear coverage, so no new tickets are required for those cases. The remaining "In Progress" relocations (Daniel Reeves, Maya Johansson, Camille Oduya, Terrence Nakamura, Andre Mitchell, Deshawn Hartley) are not genuinely stuck, their Airtable records are stale but their moves have no evidence of unresolved issues. Deshawn Hartley is a borderline case: his Airtable record is still "In Progress" and he has no Linear ticket, but his move end date (April 19) and Automate 2026 trade show deadline (April 21-24) have both passed with no evidence of problems, making completion overwhelmingly likely. The agent may optionally create a precautionary ticket for Deshawn Hartley (Mosaic Robotics, Tampa → Denver, recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver) via linear_create_issue (team: team_operations) for status reconciliation, but this is not required, the core prompt ask is satisfied by confirming existing ticket coverage for genuinely stuck items.
→ Conditional write action, if performed, goes into Outcome rubrics only.
OE 9: Send email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest about Jae-won Kim's guarantor packet.
The agent uses send_email (sender: suki.patel@moveops.com, recipients: [carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com]) following up on Jae-won Kim's Austin lease at Barton Creek Flats. Content should address the outstanding corporate guarantor packet (MoveOps entity name as guarantor, certificate of good standing, authorized signer contact, proof of business address), acknowledge the delay since the April 9 promise, and provide a timeline for delivery. This action is triggered by the investigation finding that the lease is stalled due to the missing guarantor documentation. The agent may also CC or separately notify Mina Hashimoto (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) as the StormCloud account manager.
→ Write action → goes into Outcome rubrics only.
OE 10: Post a full status rundown in #operations.
The agent uses conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006) to post a comprehensive rundown covering: (a) status of each of Suki's assigned relocations, which are confirmed completed, which are still open, and which had issues discovered; (b) what records were corrected in Airtable (status updates for relocations confirmed done); (c) whether new tracking tickets were needed in Linear and what was determined (existing coverage confirmed for Jae-won Kim, Simone Richter, Marcus Webb; Deshawn Hartley ticket created if applicable, or confirmation that no new tickets were required); (d) the StormCloud / Jae-won Kim lease status, lease is stalled due to missing guarantor packet, outreach sent to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest; (e) other issues surfaced during investigation (Simone Richter apartment mismatch being handled by Julian, Marcus Webb vehicle delay being handled by Julian).
→ Write action → goes into Outcome rubrics only.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
